justification: All 7 required rules identified with correct invariant classification. Notification rule (BR-10) correctly marked as non-invariant. Sanctions screening (BR-7) included as a hard invariant. Timezone ambiguity for daily limit explicitly surfaced as an open question. 12 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided (well above the 4 minimum). Confidence level of 78 falls within the 60-85 acceptable range with detailed assumptions listed.

observations.text: All 7 required business rules identified plus 3 additional rules (BR-3 receive capability, BR-5 partial verification limits, BR-9 support-only unlock) — thorough coverage
Notification rule (BR-10) correctly classified as non-invariant with explicit note that failure to notify must not block the transaction
Sanctions screening (BR-7) correctly identified as hard invariant with OFAC reference and compliance logging requirement
Timezone ambiguity explicitly called out in both Edge Cases and Open Questions sections — directly addresses the spec gap
12 well-structured Given/When/Then acceptance criteria covering all major rules including boundary cases (exactly $1,000, exactly $5,000)
Confidence level of 78% is well-calibrated — acknowledges known gaps without being artificially low
Correctly distinguishes partially-verified ($500) from unverified (no send) limits without conflation
Extensive edge case analysis including mid-day verification upgrade, sanctions list updates on pending transactions, and settlement day calculation
Rejection reasons section provides clear implementation guardrails tied back to specific business rules
Business impact and stakeholder analysis adds real domain depth beyond just rule enumeration

# Domain Analysis — Digital Wallet Transfer System

---

## Domain Analysis

### Business Context
This encodes the core **money movement and account security** processes for a digital wallet product. The system must enforce regulatory compliance (KYC/AML), protect users from unauthorized access, and ensure financial integrity of all transactions. This is a regulated fintech domain where correctness failures can result in legal liability, financial loss, and regulatory action.
